sections.content:
Interesting! The mouse click on N(B) at (31, 28) changed the center of N(B) from B (black) to W (white). The N(B) token is now N(W) - it has a white center instead of black.
So clicking on the center of an N token changes its center color. The N(B) token now has a W center, same as the other N token.
But wait, looking at the current state:
- id=8 color=W at [[31, 28]] - this is the center of the N token at cols 27-29, which was previously B
- id=12 color=c at [[34, 16]] - this is the center of the N token at cols 15-17, which was previously W
So clicking on (31, 28) changed the center of N(B) from B to W, AND it changed the center of N(W) from W to c (charcoal)?
Wait, let me re-check. Before the mouse click:
- N(W) token at cols 15-17 had center W at (34, 16)
- N(B) token at cols 27-29 had center B at (31, 28)
After the mouse click:
- N(W) token at cols 15-17 has center c at (34, 16)
- N(B) token at cols 27-29 has center W at (31, 28)
So clicking on one center swapped the colors? Or clicked on B and it became W, and the other one became c?
Actually, looking more carefully, the center of N(B) changed from B to W, and the center of N(W) changed from W to c. It's like the colors were transferred: the W from N(W) moved to N(B), and N(W) got a new color c.
Hmm, or maybe clicking on the center of an N token changes its color to the clicked position's color. But (31, 28) was B, and it became W...
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the mouse click on the center of N(B) "picked up" the B color and "dropped" it somewhere, or maybe it swapped the center colors between the two N tokens.
Actually wait - before the click, N(W) center was W and N(B) center was B. After the click, N(B) center is W and N(W) center is c. So it's not a simple swap.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the mouse click changes the center color to a specific color based on what was clicked. Or maybe it cycles through colors.
Actually, I think I need to reconsider the game mechanics. Let me look at the initial state again:
- Initially, N(W) had center W and N(B) had center B
- The left container has a white interior
- The right container has a white interior
Maybe the goal is to match the center colors of the N tokens to the container interiors. Both containers have white interiors, so both N tokens need white centers.
After clicking on N(B)'s center, it changed from B to W. So now both N tokens have... wait, no. N(W)'s center changed to c. So now we have N(c) and N(W).
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me try a different approach. Let me first get N(W) back into the left container, then figure out how to get N(B) into the right container.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The N(W) token is at cols 15-17, outside the container. I need to move it back to cols 12-14 to get it inside the container. Let me try LEFT.
